reasoning: As of November 9, 2025, NATO Article 5 has not been invoked. While there was a notable escalation in September 2025—during which Poland and Estonia invoked Article 4 following Russian drone and airspace incursions—it did not lead to an Article 5 declaration. In the weeks since (October and early November), NATO has maintained a measured approach, and discussions in the media treat an Article 5 invocation as an extreme, unlikely response to accidental or stray drone crossings. With only 52 days remaining in the year and no current movement toward invoking the collective defense clause, the probability of it happening before December 31, 2025, is extremely low. Given the historical rarity of Article 5 (invoked only once in over 75 years), the likelihood of it occurring in the roughly 1.5 months left drops to the minimum allowable forecast limit.
